research.product: Kubernetes API server (batch/v1 Job, list/watch protocol)
research.interface: Kubernetes REST API batch/v1 (stable), raw list+watch protocol as documented for API clients; researched against v1.36

official_sources.title: Kubernetes API Concepts
official_sources.used_for: watch=true streaming of newline-delimited WatchEvent frames (ADDED/MODIFIED/DELETED/BOOKMARK/ERROR), allowWatchBookmarks, resourceVersion semantics for list and watch, 410 Gone / reason Expired recovery by relisting, and the meta/v1 Status error kind

official_sources.title: Job | Kubernetes (kubernetes-api reference)
official_sources.used_for: batch/v1 GET /apis/batch/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/jobs list and watch paths, JobList shape, JobStatus.active/succeeded/failed counters, and JobCondition semantics (Complete/Failed/Suspended with status True)

official_sources.title: Kubernetes v1.36 official OpenAPI specification (kubernetes/kubernetes, release-1.36)
official_sources.used_for: exact query parameters of the namespaced Job list/watch operation (watch, allowWatchBookmarks, resourceVersion, resourceVersionMatch), the WatchEvent {type,object} definition, JobCondition fields, and Status fields (status/message/reason/code)

research.language_rationale: client-go is the official Go SDK, but this integration deliberately targets the documented raw list/watch HTTP protocol: kubernetes.io 'API Concepts' specifies it as the supported contract for API clients, and it is exactly what client-go's reflector implements underneath. Go's stdlib net/http plus a streaming encoding/json decoder is the idiomatic zero-dependency way to implement it, and the harness forbids third-party modules.

verified_facts: Namespaced Jobs are listed with GET /apis/batch/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/jobs; the JobList response carries metadata.resourceVersion, which is the version a subsequent watch should start from.

A watch is the same GET with watch=true (watch=1 is also accepted); the response is a chunked stream of newline-delimited JSON WatchEvent objects {"type": ADDED|MODIFIED|DELETED|BOOKMARK|ERROR, "object": ...}.

allowWatchBookmarks=true requests BOOKMARK events; a bookmark's object carries only metadata.resourceVersion and marks that all changes up to that version have been sent; servers may ignore the flag, so clients must not rely on bookmark cadence.

When the requested resourceVersion has been compacted away the server answers 410 Gone — either as the HTTP status of the watch request or as an ERROR event whose object is a Status with code 410 and reason Expired; the documented recovery is a fresh list followed by a new watch from the returned resourceVersion.
Leaving resourceVersion unset on a list request gives Most Recent semantics (a consistent, quorum-read snapshot), which is what the post-410 relist uses.
API errors are meta/v1 Status objects: kind Status, status Failure, plus message, reason and code fields.

JobStatus.conditions marks a finished Job with a condition of type Complete (success) or Failed with status "True"; Suspended and SuccessCriteriaMet conditions do not make a Job terminal; status.active/succeeded/failed are pod counts.
